citations.description: Epic decomposition children: ed13, 555e, 0b96, 9a5a, 18e6, 3c07, 7931, 2f8e, 5062, c595, 3275 — none can be status-checked from file tools (tickets branch data not accessible)

findings.criterion: All child tickets are closed (epic rollup), verified by `rebar list --epic <this> --status open` returning none.
findings.detail: The epic itself has status `in_progress` (confirmed via show_ticket). AC1 requires all child tickets to be closed, verified by running `rebar list --epic 86ac-086a-20ad-4327 --status open` and getting an empty result. I cannot access the tickets branch data (the event store) from my file tools, so I cannot positively verify that every child ticket referenced in the decomposition (ed13, 555e, 0b96, 9a5a, 18e6, 3c07, 7931, 2f8e, 5062, c595, 3275, etc.) is in a `closed` state. The epic being `in_progress` rather than `closed` is consistent with children remaining open. No codebase file provides a positive assertion that all children are closed.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
